files.scope: data types, type classification, serialization, materialization
semantic types, interfaces, domains, delegates, enforcement
design principles, type system overview, classification axes, subordination, levels
AST grammar, declaration normatives, canonical syntax examples per construct
behavioral properties, interaction constraints, normative properties per construct
philosophical foundations, theses from introduction, meaning-first paradigm, semantic decay, anti-drift
formal T&D terms, language constructs (primitives, blocks, streams, derived, behavioral, semantic), block syntax keywords, annex definitions
ontological role index: concept × WHAT/WHY/HOW/WHEN. Routes questions by ontological type to aspect-level chain addresses. 67 units across 22 constructs

unitName.role: Higher-order keyword. Provides orthogonal classification for the keywords[] array. Each word in the name is also a keyword that may match other units — forming reading chains across units.
unitName.parsing: camelCase → split on capitals; snake_case → split on underscores. Result = classification keywords.
unitName.chaining: Each word from unitName can be a keyword in ANOTHER unit. This creates implicit reading chains: unitA.name contains word X → X is keyword in unitB → unitB becomes part of the reading plan. This is how the index builds cross-unit reading sequences without explicit $ref.

unitStructure.keywords: Low-order keywords. Specific terms within the unit's domain. Used for fine-grained matching.
unitStructure.seq: Ordered reading chain. Each entry has Order (reading position), H{N} field (heading level+title), chain[] (addresses).

headingField.naming: Field name = H1, H2, H3, H4, H5, H6. Encodes both heading level and title text.

normalization.rule: Value MAY start with a section/annex number prefix. Strip ^[A-Z0-9][\d.]*\s+ to get pure heading text for rg search.
normalization.search: rg -i '^#{level}\s+.*{STRIPPED}' file.md — where {level} = repeat # by field number (H2 → ##)

unitStructure.$ref: Intra-index reference to another unit. Expands in-place during reading.

readingPlanMechanisms.explicit: seq[].Order + seq[].chain[] + seq[].$ref — direct reading plan defined by the unit author.
readingPlanMechanisms.implicit: unitName words → match as keywords in other units → those units extend the reading plan. This mechanism works ALONGSIDE explicit seq/order/chain, not instead of it.

crossRefIndex.scope: Section cross-references graph: outgoing links from each section to other sections, external standards (RFC, ISO, GDPR, …), URLs, and person contacts

weight.description: xref-derived chains are supplementary context — they follow outgoing links from already-matched sections. They rank below all primary index matches but above unstructured fulltext search.

scoring.metaRank: last+1 — xref chains get metaRank = (number of matched aspect indices) + 1. They always appear after primary matches in the reading plan.
scoring.refTypeWeightsNote: Added to base score. section refs are most navigable (0 penalty), external standards carry a small penalty (5), URLs and persons are least actionable (10).

weight.tierPlacement: xref chains typically land in Tier 3 (Registry & cross-refs) or Tier 4 (Supplementary context) depending on the originating section's rank and order.
weight.integration: NOT auto-included in search_spec. Used on-demand: (1) agent reads xref for a matched section to discover related sections, (2) agent manually adds discovered chains to the reading plan via read_chain tool.

numberingRules.levelFromHash: # count = heading level: # = H1, ## = H2, ### = H3, #### = H4

typeDetection.rule: Read first token after # marks. First character determines type.
typeDetection.letter: ANNEX section. Numbering: A.1.2... Search in annex_*.md files.

genericSections.rule: Generic marker .0. is detected ONLY relative to document base chain, NOT absolute numbering.
algorithm: 1. Determine document base chain from H1 title (e.g. 2.5, 2.7, 4.0, 2.0)
2. Strip base chain prefix from section number
3. If the FIRST remaining segment is 0 → section is GENERIC, applies to all siblings
4. A 0 that is part of the base chain itself is NOT a generic marker
genericSections.readingRule: When reading X.Y.N (N≠0), ALWAYS first read X.Y.0.* generic sections from the same or parent document.

baseChainFromFilename.rule: Filename digit groups → base chain. If 3+ digit groups and last is 0, strip it — file is generic for parent.

pipeline: 1. SECTION TYPE: determine target section type(s) from query (see intents)
4. Filter by section type using sectionTitles.*.rg
6. chain → section.search → find section in file
8. Assemble in ORDER. No reorder. No summarize.

canonical_example.note: Code block ONLY. No semantics, no normative.

normative_rules.note: CHAIN_ONLY. ALL normative subsections. NEVER summarize.

full.note: Follow chain ORDER. Skip nothing. No normative summaries.

normativeRules.NO_SUMMARIZE: NEVER summarize normative. Quote as-is or combine as validation chains.
normativeRules.CHAIN_ONLY: if(condition) → then(requirement). Chain order = validation order.

blockRules.ORDER_MATTERS: Chain array order = mandatory reading order.
blockRules.ASPECTS_ARE_ATOMIC: Each aspect (A., B., C.) is atomic. Never merge across sections.

Semantic Role.note: WHAT. Identity/purpose. Aspects (A., B., C.) are atomic.

Normative.note: CHAIN_ONLY. Always numbered lists. Always read ENTIRE section.

Syntax.note: HOW. Code blocks verbatim. Declaration subsections are normative.

Designation.note: WHAT. Name, category, form. Short, factual.

Classification.note: WHAT. Categories/axes. Often at H1/H2. Preserve hierarchy.

Reference.note: CITATION/RESOURCE. Links to other sections, external standards, persons. Mandatory reading before proceeding.

Note.note: WHY/HOW. Supplementary clarification. Blockquote or titled section. Context-only, no new requirements.

ontological.unitOverrides: 15 units have explicit onto overrides in their index files (ont: 8, desa: 4, sema: 3). onma has per-unit onto by design — each unit is a concept×role combination.

nodeRoles.H3: HOW/WHEN — details for H2, contains Aspect
nodeRoles.Note: WHY/HOW — supplementary, applies to any parent
nodeRoles.Aspect: HOW/WHEN — orthogonal sub-aspect inside H1-H4, never standalone

grammar.note: Quick reference. Full validation map with $ref navigation: structure_map.json
grammar.hierarchy: Canvas > Section(H1-H6) > {Paragraph, List, Aspect, CodeBlock, Note, Reference}
